title: Hadoop MapReduce Architecture
description: The Hadoop MapReduce architecture is a distributed processing framework that allows for the processing of large data sets across clusters of computers. It consists of a master node, which coordinates the tasks and distributes the data, and multiple slave nodes, which execute the tasks and store the data. The master node assigns tasks to the slave nodes based on their availability and the data location, and monitors the progress of the tasks. The data is stored in a distributed file system, which allows for efficient access and sharing of data across the cluster. The architecture is designed to be scalable, fault-tolerant, and efficient, and it supports a wide range of applications, including web search, data mining, and machine learning.
raw_response:
TITLE: Hadoop MapReduce Architecture

DESCRIPTION:
The Hadoop MapReduce architecture is a distributed processing framework that allows for the processing of large data sets across clusters of computers. It consists of a master node, which coordinates the tasks and distributes the data, and multiple slave nodes, which execute the tasks and store the data. The master node assigns tasks to the slave nodes based on their availability and the data location, and monitors the progress of the tasks. The data is stored in a distributed file system, which allows for efficient access and sharing of data across the cluster. The architecture is designed to be scalable, fault-tolerant, and efficient, and it supports a wide range of applications, including web search, data mining, and machine learning.
